decisions.decision: Ran both quick tasks inline (not worktree subagents)
decisions.rationale: Runtime full-stack/Docker boots are hard for a detached agent to monitor and worktree isolation gives nothing for shared Docker resources

decisions.decision: Sandbox profile pairs real Azampay gateway with the always-true stub signature validator
decisions.rationale: Lets the live sandbox flow run before HMAC scheme exists; safe due to utilityRef DB check + idempotent crediting; prod stays blocked

next_action: Run /gsd-audit-milestone, then /gsd-complete-milestone — flag Phase 6 store submission as the single external open item. Alternatively pick up a Phase 0 dev deliverable (prod HmacSignatureValidator once the scheme is known, or the Terraform).
context_notes: All 7 implementation phases are complete; tree clean at 9e79dfc. This session: resumed + reconciled a stale STATE.md, closed Phase 7's DEVX-01 human_needed item via a real full-stack smoke test, then added a `sandbox` Spring profile so the real Azampay sandbox flow is exercisable without prod onboarding/HMAC. Two quick tasks recorded in STATE 'Quick Tasks Completed'. Known dev-tooling follow-ups (logged, non-blocking): start.sh/stop.sh leave gradle-forked JVMs orphaned on their ports (killing the bootRun launcher PID doesn't kill the child); start.sh has no port pre-flight check.
